title: ATAH Profession Category
description: ATAH Registry Operation Profile object: profession category metadata record (spec/v0.8 §4.4). Each category declares its own `band_definitions` (name, position, threshold rule); ordering within the band uses a documented fairness policy. The category's `review_signal_band_cap` records the influence of review-derived signals on band assignment for the category, preserving the safeguard that review platforms do not dominate matching in regulated contexts. The matching engine that consumes these fields is documented in §9 of `full-spec.md`. Required for implementations claiming Registry Conformance.

band_definitions.description: Per-category band specifications used by the §9 matching engine. Each band has a name, a position (lower = higher band; a candidate in band_position 1 appears before a candidate in band_position 2 in the response), and a threshold rule describing the predicate a candidate must satisfy to be in this band. The threshold-rule grammar is implementation-defined for v0.8.2 (a free-text predicate using documented signals); v0.9 may standardise it. The default bands across launch categories are `verification_confidence`, `category_fit`, `availability_window`, and `contact_freshness`; categories MAY define more or different bands as long as the band assignment behaviour is observable through `match-response.schema.json` `band_assignment`.

review_signal_band_cap.description: Per F-7, this field replaces the v0.8.1 `review_signal_weight_cap`. The cap preserves the original safeguard under the band-based model: review-derived signals MAY supplement transparency and confidence metadata, but for high-stakes regulated categories MUST NOT move a candidate into a higher eligibility or verification band unless corroborated by authoritative credential or regulator-source evidence. High-stakes regulated categories set `may_upgrade_band: false` and `regulated_category_max_effect: no_band_upgrade`. Lower-stakes categories MAY permit `may_upgrade_band: true` with documented authoritative-corroboration requirements.

contact_verification_cadence.description: Per-category cadence for Layer 1 periodic contact-channel verification (per spec §12A). High-stakes regulated categories (credentialled tier with mandatory body membership) typically use `quarterly`; mid-stakes regulated `biannual`; established practitioners `annual`. Categories without a value use the protocol-level default of `annual`. The cadence drives when ATAH issues verification challenges to the professional's `notification_channels`; missed challenges escalate per §12A Layer 2 and may flip `matching_status` to `contact_unverified`. The field is per-category configurable so launch categories can be tuned individually without a schema change.

pre_handoff_freshness_window_days.description: Per-category recency window (in days) for Layer 3 pre-handoff freshness check (per spec §12A). Only populated for high-stakes categories where a just-in-time verification before sending the introduction notification is warranted. v0.8.2 ships with 30 as the populated value for high-stakes categories. Absent for categories that do not require Layer 3 (the protocol does not run Layer 3 for those categories).
